name: FlatWhite Design System
description: Design system extracted from FlatWhite consulting site — minimalist, editorial, premium-consulting aesthetic with subtle motion graphics.

logo.style: Light/Regular weight sans-serif with terminating period as a brand accent
logo.treatment: Always lowercase 'lat' and 'hite' with capital F and W, period included

brand.voice: Premium, calm, declarative. Short sentences. Outcome-focused (growth, clarity, measurable results).

slate.comment: Used on Process and FAQ sections — desaturated blue-grey panels

accent_usage.principle: Accent only on interactive elements and accent lines. Never as text color in titles or body.
use_for: Hover state op primary buttons (subtle shift, niet hele kleur-wissel)
Active nav indicator (1px underline of dot)
Hover state op vacature cards: 1px accent border-top of bottom line
Decoratieve 1px lijntjes als section dividers waar passend
Hover state op text links (underline kleur)

display.stack: 'Neue Haas Grotesk Display', 'Inter Display', 'Helvetica Neue', system-ui, sans-serif
display.comment: Tight, geometric sans for headlines — slightly condensed feel

display-xl.usage: Hero headlines (Empowering companies to grow smarter and faster)

display-lg.usage: Section heroes (Where strategy, operations and innovation meet measurable results)

display-md.usage: Section titles (Straightforward answers, Unlock growth with strategic insights)

pill.usage: Category pills (/ OUR EXPERTS, / FUTURE-READY BUSINESS, / WHAT WE OFFER)

radii.comment: Pills (nav buttons, category labels) are fully rounded. Cards use ~20px. Outer section panels use ~32px.

shadows.comment: Site is largely flat — shadows used sparingly. Depth comes from background contrast, not shadows.

pill.comment: All section labels use a slash prefix — distinctive brand element

processStep.comment: Each step is laid out diagonally across the slate panel, connected by the animated thin curving line
processStep.title: h3 white
processStep.spacing: Steps offset vertically — 001 right-bottom, 002 left-bottom, 003 right-middle

principles: Scroll-driven reveals — content emerges as it enters the viewport.
Line drawing is the signature motion — used in the Process section to connect steps.

easings.comment-standard: easeOutExpo-style — fast start, gentle settle. Used for most reveals.
easings.comment-smooth: Symmetric ease for path drawing and continuous motion.

scrollLineDraw.name: Process section animated thin line
scrollLineDraw.description: A single 1px white/translucent line draws itself through the three process steps as the user scrolls. The line curves at each step's corner — straight horizontal, then a quarter-circle bend, then another straight, weaving between steps 001, 002, and 003.

implementation.technique: SVG path with stroke-dasharray + stroke-dashoffset animated via scroll progress
implementation.driver: scroll position within section bounding box (0 → 1)
implementation.library: Framer Motion useScroll + useTransform, or GSAP ScrollTrigger, or scroll-timeline CSS

exampleCode.framerMotion: const { scrollYProgress } = useScroll({ target: sectionRef, offset: ['start end', 'end start'] }); const pathLength = useTransform(scrollYProgress, [0.15, 0.85], [0, 1]); <motion.path d={d} style={{ pathLength }} stroke='rgba(255,255,255,0.35)' strokeWidth={1} fill='none' />
exampleCode.css: @supports (animation-timeline: scroll()) { .line-path { animation: draw linear; animation-timeline: scroll(); stroke-dashoffset: 0; } @keyframes draw { from { stroke-dashoffset: var(--length); } to { stroke-dashoffset: 0; } } }

accents.endpointDots: Small 4px circles fade in at each step corner as the line reaches them
accents.glow: Optional subtle blur(8px) duplicate of the path at 20% opacity for premium feel

headlineReveal.name: Large headline word-by-word reveal
headlineReveal.description: Hero and section headlines reveal word-by-word from a slight Y-offset and 0 opacity.

buttonHover.background: subtle lightening on dark / darkening on light

navBlurOnScroll.description: Header background opacity goes 0 → 0.92 with backdrop-blur as user scrolls past 64px

barChartGrow.name: Hero stat-bar chart growth
barChartGrow.description: The four labeled bars (ANALYSIS, MARKET, GROWTH, STRATEGY, PLANNING) grow from height 0 to final height with staggered timing on scroll-into-view.
barChartGrow.labelFade: Bar labels fade in after their bar finishes growing (delay 200ms)

faqAccordionOpen.heightAuto: animated via grid-template-rows: 0fr → 1fr trick, or framer-motion height auto
faqAccordionOpen.iconRotation: Plus icon rotates 45° to become X over 250ms

marqueeTrustBar.name: Trusted-by avatars subtle drift
marqueeTrustBar.description: The small grouped avatars next to '/ 5.0' can have a very subtle continuous horizontal drift (3px range over 4s ease-in-out infinite alternate)

scrollBehavior.comment: Site benefits from inertia scrolling — pairs with the slow, drawn line animation.

sectionStructure.patternA: Pill-label (top) + large left-aligned headline (3 lines, dropped baseline) — used for hero and section openers
sectionStructure.patternB: Pill-label (top-left or centered) + center-aligned headline — used for FAQ
sectionStructure.patternC: Process layout: massive headline anchored top-left, three offset content blocks distributed diagonally, connected by the animated curving line

iconography.style: Outline, 1.5px stroke, rounded caps and joins

portraits.treatment: Plain neutral background (#F2F2F2), business-casual subjects, soft natural lighting, slight desaturation

hero.treatment: Top-down overhead shots of collaborative work scenes — warm wood tones, leather, laptops, plants. Cinematic, document-style.
hero.overlay: Slight darkening gradient (rgba(0,0,0,0.15) → 0) from center for text legibility

signaturePatterns: Slash-prefixed pills (/ LABEL) used as section markers throughout
Numbered indices (/ 001, / 002) for sequential content — process steps and service rows
Massive type with tight tracking and generous line-height — editorial, slightly oversized
Slate-blue (#6B7384) panels as the secondary surface — adds tonal variation without color noise
Animated curving 1px line connecting process steps (the signature scroll-driven motion)
Pill-shaped CTAs in both filled (black) and outlined variants, always paired in the nav
Service list as expandable accordion rows with numbered indices and trailing plus icons

implementationNotes.recommendedStack: Framer or Next.js + Framer Motion + Tailwind + Lenis for smooth scroll
implementationNotes.criticalMotionElement: The process-section scroll-driven line is THE signature interaction. Invest implementation time here — use SVG path with pathLength tied to useScroll for the cleanest result.
implementationNotes.performance: Avoid animating box-shadow or filter on scroll. Stick to transform and opacity. Use will-change sparingly.
